evals.prompt: I need to set up GitHub Actions to run terraform plan on PRs and terraform apply when they merge to main. We use GCP and our Terraform is in the infrastructure/ directory. Make sure it's secure.
evals.expected_output: Two workflow files: terraform-plan.yml triggered on pull_request with path filter, and terraform-apply.yml triggered on push to main. Both use GCP Workload Identity Federation via google-github-actions/auth (not service account keys). Actions pinned by SHA. Plan workflow has cancel-in-progress: true, apply has cancel-in-progress: false. Both use -input=false. Plan output posted as PR comment using env vars (not direct interpolation). Permissions block restricts GITHUB_TOKEN scope with id-token: write.
assertions: Plan workflow triggers on pull_request with paths filter for infrastructure/
Apply workflow triggers on push to main with paths filter for infrastructure/
Authentication uses google-github-actions/auth with workload_identity_provider (not service account key)
id-token: write permission is present
All actions are pinned by commit SHA, not by tag
Apply workflow concurrency has cancel-in-progress: false
All terraform commands include -input=false flag
PR comment uses env variable for plan output, not direct interpolation in run block
permissions block is present and restrictive (contents: read at minimum)

evals.prompt: We have a monorepo with Terragrunt. Our environments are in infrastructure/terragrunt/us-central1/dev/ and infrastructure/terragrunt/us-central1/prod/. I want dev to auto-apply on merge but prod to require manual approval. Set up the GitHub Actions workflows.
evals.expected_output: Workflows that handle multi-environment Terragrunt deployments. Dev applies automatically on merge to main. Prod requires GitHub Environment protection with required reviewers. Uses reusable workflow pattern or separate jobs with needs: dependency. Terragrunt-specific setup (gruntwork-io/setup-terragrunt or manual install). Caches terragrunt and terraform plugins. Each environment has its own concurrency group.
assertions: Workflow handles both dev and prod environments
Dev environment applies automatically (no manual gate)
Prod environment uses GitHub Environment protection with required reviewers or explicit approval step
Prod job depends on dev job (needs: or sequential)
Terragrunt is installed (setup action or manual)
Each environment has a separate concurrency group
Caching is configured for terraform plugins and/or terragrunt cache

evals.prompt: Add drift detection for our Terraform infrastructure. It should run on a schedule, check if real infrastructure has drifted from the code, and open a GitHub issue if drift is found.
evals.expected_output: A scheduled workflow that runs terraform plan -detailed-exitcode. Exit code 2 indicates drift. On drift, creates a GitHub issue with the plan output. Uses WIF for auth. Includes the plan output in the issue body (truncated if needed). Labels the issue appropriately.
assertions: Workflow uses schedule trigger with cron expression
Uses terraform plan -detailed-exitcode to detect drift
Checks exit code 2 specifically (not just non-zero)
Creates a GitHub issue when drift is detected
Issue body includes plan output
Authentication uses OIDC/WIF (not static credentials)
issues: write permission is present
